mnemonics.title: Objects Come Home
mnemonics.content: Objects Come Home: A method can return an object, just like it can return a primitive. Imagine a method as a factory that sends a complete product (object) back to the caller, not just a single part (primitive).

mnemonics.title: REFER
mnemonics.content: REFER: Returning Existing or Freshly created Entities Regularly. A method can return a new or existing object reference.

mnemonics.title: PASSED
mnemonics.content: PASSED: Primitive types Are Sent Separately, Ensuring Data is Detached. Objects are passed by reference, while primitives are passed by value.
